name: Osso Buco
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the veal shanks generously with salt and pepper.
2. Heat olive oil and butter in a large Dutch oven over medium heat. Quickly sear the veal on all sides until browned, then transfer to paper towels to drain excess fat.
3. Add chopped onions, garlic, basil, and oregano to the pan; cook over medium for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Add tomatoes, salt, and pepper to taste; continue cooking for another 10 minutes. Skim any foam off the surface.
5. Pour in white wine, increase heat, and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and let simmer, uncovered, for 15 minutes.
6. Return veal to the pan, add enough stock to cover the meat, then cover and simmer for 1 to 1½ hours. Alternatively, bake at 350°F (175°C) for the same duration; remove lid and continue cooking until veal is tender.
7. Just before serving, sprinkle with lemon zest and chopped parsley. Traditional accompaniment: Risotto alla Milanese. Consider trying small potatoes tossed with olive oil and garlic or pasta prepared similarly.
